# Preface {.unnumbered}

This is the operator manual for the **Live Reactivation Decoder** — a
standalone PyQt6 application that trains an EEG decoder offline and then runs
it against a live data stream in real time.

The app has two phases:

- **Phase 1 (offline):** an operator-driven workflow that loads a recording,
  preprocesses it, evaluates candidate decoding timepoints, and trains the
  final decoder, producing a `decoder_pipeline.joblib` artifact.
- **Phase 2 (online):** real-time inference against a Lab Streaming Layer
  (LSL) stream, consuming the artifact produced by Phase 1.

## Who this manual is for

This manual is written for the **operator** running an experiment session —
the person sitting at the decoding laptop. It assumes you can open a terminal
and run commands, but it does not assume you know the internals of the
decoding pipeline.

For architecture and developer documentation, see the references under
[`docs/`](../) instead.

## How to read this manual

- If you are setting up a new machine, start with **Getting started**
  (@sec-overview through @sec-hardware).
- If the machine is already set up and you just need to run a session, jump to
  **Using the app** (@sec-phase1 and @sec-phase2).
- Keep **Reference** (configuration and troubleshooting) close by during a
  session.
